on Sundays.


The first news of the new schedule
was received by Local Chairman
Johnson, which read: New schedule
is signed. No friction"


A copy of the new schedule is now
en- route to Spokanes 1t goes into
effect February 10. All operators
were rejoicing yesterday over the
success of the Order of Railway
Photographers and many kilograms of
congratulation We're sent to II. B..
Pcrham, president of the union. for
his successful campaign.


The Northern Pacific is the next
line with which negotiations will be
opened and meeting will take place
in st. Paul today. 1t is probable the
Northern Pacific will grant the de-
mands of the men, generally conceded
to be reasonable.


The general committee. which has
been in st. Paul over five weeks, has
adjourned and the members are re-
turning to their respective divisions.
